lesson: A hill makes an angle θ with the horizontal surface. A stickman of mass m starts from rest and walks up the hill, as shown. Assume the coefficient of kinetic friction between the stickman and the hill is μ_k, and take g = 9.8 m/s^2. Let x be the distance measured along the incline from the starting point. (a) Find Fnet, the magnitude of the net force acting on the stickman along the incline. (b) Find t_x, the time for the stickman to reach position x on the incline.
